methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Stark Defence
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

private.name: Vatn Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $300M

private.name: Ares Industries
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

data.narrative:
Swift Beat, a Series A-stage Defense & Security company founded by Eric Schmidt, operates within a competitive peer set of 10 private and 2 public comparables, including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries at the same stage.

Two valuation methods anchor this analysis. Stage-progression, using the company's $50M total raised as a proxy for post-money valuation, applies a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $100M. Peer-median analysis across the three identified same-stage, same-sector comparables produces a $300M implied value, with peer valuations ranging from $150M (Ares Industries) to $500M (Stark Defence) and a median of $300M.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $85M to $345M, with a median point of $200M. The 4x spread between methods reflects material uncertainty in relative positioning within the Defense & Security cohort. Key drivers of variance include differentiation on technical capability relative to Stark Defence ($500M) versus earlier-stage positioning closer to Ares Industries ($150M), capital efficiency compared to peers, and near-term revenue inflection risk. The $50M+ raised to date provides a floor, but peer heterogeneity at Series A stage creates substantial uncertainty around which comp set best anchors fair value.
